title: Little Women; Or, Meg, Jo, Beth, and Amy
author: Alcott, Louisa May
summary: Little Women follows the daily life of the four March sisters—Meg, Jo, Beth, and Amy—during a modest Christmas while their father is away with the army. Facing poverty, they debate forgoing presents to support the war effort, reveal personal wishes (books, music, drawing supplies), and quarrel gently about chores and school. Mother guides them toward sacrifice and mutual support. The sisters’ personalities emerge: Jo the bookish tomboy, Meg the domestic dreamer, Beth the gentle peacemaker, Amy the vain younger artist. Warm household detail and moral reflection frame their bonds, plans, and hopes for future happiness together.

characters.name: Jo March
characters.personality: bookish, outspoken, tomboyish, impatient with domestic tedium
characters.lifespan: Remains at home with her family in the excerpt, grappling with poverty and longing for books and independence

characters.name: Meg March
characters.personality: responsible, domestic-minded, longs for pretty things and social pleasures
characters.lifespan: Stays at home in the excerpt, teaching and managing household duties while yearning for nicer clothes

characters.name: Beth March
characters.lifespan: Remains quietly supportive and content in the household in the excerpt, aiding family morale

characters.name: Amy March
characters.personality: vain about appearance, artistically inclined, somewhat spoiled
characters.lifespan: Lives at home in the excerpt, focusing on drawing and personal improvement

characters.name: Mother (Mrs. March)
characters.lifespan: Acts as the household's moral center and caretaker in the excerpt, proposing sacrifices for the war effort

characters.name: Father (Mr. March)
characters.personality: absent but respected; serving in the army
characters.lifespan: Absent from home and serving in the army in the excerpt; his return is uncertain in the presented text
